ip_outcome_note: Depicted ghost inhabiting a decorative teapot matches Polteageist's canonical design exactly. National Pokedex no. 855. Stage 1 evolution from Sinistea confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-83).

trivia_bullets.text: Polteageist (National Pokedex no. 855) is a Stage 1 Ghost-type introduced in Generation VIII (Sword & Shield), evolving from Sinistea. Two forms exist: Phony Form (common; Sinistea has no authenticity stamp, evolves with a Cracked Pot) and Antique Form (rare; Sinistea bears a tiny blue authenticity mark on the inner rim of the cup, evolves with a Chipped Pot). The TCG card does not distinguish form — both forms share identical stats, moves, and abilities. [WebSearch snippet: pokemondb.net/pokebase/349628]

Flavor text: "This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found." — directly references the Antique/Phony form split in the games. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-83]

The Mad Party attack (20x damage per Mad Party attacker in the discard pile) is a multi-card combo mechanic shared across Darkness Ablaze with Bunnelby, Dedenne, Galarian Mr. Mime, and Galarian Mr. Rime — all carry Mad Party. Polteageist also has the Tea Break ability, which lets the player discard a Mad Party Pokemon from hand to draw 2 cards, making it the deck's engine and not just a beatstick. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-83]

This card follows DAA-082 Sinistea (already in the inventory, trivia'd in wave 23), completing the Sinistea-Polteageist line across two sequential card numbers in the same set — both Psychic-type, both illustrated by the same artist kirisAki. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-82, swsh3-83]
